REASONING:
In Python, the equivalent type for a 2D array of strings is `List[Tuple[str]]`. This is because in Java, a String[][] represents an array of arrays (or matrix), where each inner array can have different lengths. In contrast, in Python, we typically use lists to represent arrays and tuples to represent fixed-length sequences. Therefore, the equivalent type for `String[][]` in Python would be `List[Tuple[str]]`.

JAVA CODE:
```
    public static void compare(
            final String message, final String[][] expected, final List<CSVRecord> actual) {
        final int expectedLength = expected.length;
        assertEquals(expectedLength, actual.size(), message + "  - outer array size");
        for (int i = 0; i < expectedLength; i++) {
            assertArrayEquals(expected[i], actual.get(i).values(), message + " (entry " + i + ")");
        }
    }
```
